overview: The Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee approved two rezoning petitions and voted to forward a modified fee waiver resolution to the county board that will only waive reconnect fees for septic systems on properties damaged by the April 17, 2026 tornado. The committee also received department overviews from Solid Waste, Parks Recreation and Forestry, and Conservation Planning and Zoning.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Public hearing: Tim Veland rezoning petition (urban residential/general agriculture to rural residential) - Town of Green Valley
Public hearing: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition (conservancy recreation to general agriculture) - Town of McMillan
Consideration of fee waiver for properties impacted by April 17, 2026 severe weather
Department overview: Parks, Recreation, and Forestry
Department overview: Conservation, Planning, and Zoning

discussions.body: Supervisor Sefeld made a motion to approve the minutes, seconded by Supervisor Drebeck. The motion carried with no opposition.

discussions.item: Tim Veland rezoning petition - Town of Green Valley
discussions.body: Garrett Pogle presented the staff report for rezoning from urban residential and general agriculture to rural residential for a proposed land division. The Town of Green Valley recommended approval on March 10, 2026. No public testimony was offered for, against, or as interested. Supervisor Drebeck moved to approve based on findings of fact, conclusions of law, and town approval, seconded by Supervisor Kurt. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition - Town of McMillan
discussions.body: The petition sought to rezone from conservancy recreation to general agriculture. Supervisor Leur asked about the conservancy nature of the property; Garrett Pogle explained the property contains a home and horse riding stable, and the rezone would make the existing structures conforming uses rather than non-conforming. No natural areas would be lost. The Town of McMillan recommended approval on April 20, 2026. No public testimony was offered. Supervisor Sefelt moved to approve and forward to county board, seconded by Supervisor Drew. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Fee waiver for properties impacted by April 17, 2026 severe weather
discussions.body: Director Lori Mskimmons presented a proposal to waive county zoning and septic fees for tornado-damaged properties in the towns of Ringle, Easton, and villages of Kronenwetter and Weston. Supervisor Drew questioned where to draw the line for future events. Supervisor Drebeck expressed concerns about fairness to property owners affected by smaller disasters not declared emergencies. After significant debate, Supervisor Sefelt moved to forward the resolution, seconded by Supervisor Leur. Supervisor Drebeck then amended the motion to only waive reconnect fees for septic systems (not other zoning or septic fees), seconded by Supervisor Kurt. The amendment passed. The amended motion to forward to county board passed but was not unanimous.

Director Dave Hagen Busher presented on solid waste operations, explaining the landfill accepts about 1,000 tons daily, has partnerships with Portage and Shawano counties, and began injecting renewable natural gas into pipelines in September 2025 through a partnership with Vidi Energy. He highlighted the upcoming $6 million 11-acre construction project and the need for on-site leachate treatment by 2032 due to new state requirements (Acts 200 and 201). Marilyn Bend praised the department for helping keep private landfill costs in line.

discussions.item: Department overview: Parks, Recreation, and Forestry
discussions.body: Tom Lovelin, County Forest Administrator, presented on behalf of Director Jamie who had an emergency. He explained the department operates both county and city park systems with 44 full-time and 120 seasonal employees. A Nine Mile County Forest study showed out-of-town visitors generate $3.25 million for the Marathon County economy. Tom announced his retirement effective June 1st after nearly 30 years with the county. Supervisor Sefelt praised the department and Tom's service.

actionItems: Rezoning petition for James Offer and David Tract (urban residential/general agriculture to rural residential) approved and forwarded to county board
Rezoning petition for Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz (conservancy recreation to general agriculture) approved and forwarded to county board
Modified fee waiver resolution (reconnect fees only) for tornado-damaged properties forwarded to county board for consideration
Towns should update comprehensive plans to reflect zoning changes if applicable
title: Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee Meeting
